items.text:
Subject: Join the Astronomy Club's Spring Stargazing Night

Dear Students,

The Riverside Astronomy Club invites you to our annual Spring Stargazing Night on Friday, April 18, from 8:00 PM to 11:00 PM at the Maple Hill Observatory. This year's theme is the spring constellations, and Professor Alvarez will give a short guided tour of the night sky before we open the telescopes.

There's a $5 fee to help cover equipment rental and hot drinks, which you can pay at the door or online through the student portal by April 15. Members who registered before March pay nothing. Please bring a warm jacket, since the observatory deck stays cool after dark. We'll provide blankets and folding chairs.

If the weather looks cloudy, we'll move the event indoors to Carver Hall, Room 110, for a planetarium show instead. Either way, it'll be a great evening.

We hope to see you there. Questions? Just reply to this email.

Best regards,
Dana Whitfield
Events Coordinator, Riverside Astronomy Club

format_metadata.subject: Join the Astronomy Club's Spring Stargazing Night

questions.stem: According to the email, what should students who are not early members do to attend?

options.A: Reserve a telescope through Professor Alvarez before the tour.
options.B: Pay a $5 fee at the door or online before April 15.
options.C: Register with the student portal during the month of March.
options.D: Bring folding chairs and warm blankets to the observatory.

questions.explanation: The email states there is a $5 fee that can be paid at the door or online through the student portal by April 15. Members who registered before March pay nothing, so non-early members must pay the fee.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the Maple Hill Observatory deck?

options.A: It offers a clearer view of stars than Carver Hall does.
options.B: It becomes uncomfortably cold for visitors at night.
options.C: It charges an extra fee for using the telescopes.
options.D: It remains open to students throughout the spring term.

questions.explanation: The email advises bringing a warm jacket because the observatory deck stays cool after dark, implying the deck gets cold and uncomfortable at night.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To explain how the student portal handles event payments.
options.B: To announce a change in the Astronomy Club's leadership.
options.C: To invite students to a club stargazing event and its details.
options.D: To describe the spring constellations visible this season.

questions.explanation: The email's overall purpose is to invite students to the Spring Stargazing Night and provide details such as time, location, fee, and the rain plan. The other options capture only single details or unstated ideas.

items.text:
Subject: Your Writing Center Appointment Is Confirmed

Hi Marcus,

This email confirms your tutoring appointment at the Bennett Writing Center for Tuesday, May 6, from 2:00 PM to 2:45 PM in Room 308 of Lowell Library. Your tutor will be Priya Nair, who specializes in research papers and citation formatting.

Please bring a printed copy of your draft along with the assignment instructions so Priya can review the requirements with you. If you'd like feedback on a specific section, mark it ahead of time. Sessions begin promptly, so try to arrive a few minutes early to check in at the front desk.

If you need to cancel, please notify us at least 24 hours in advance through the online scheduling system. Late cancellations or no-shows may limit your access to future same-week appointments.

We look forward to working with you.

Thanks,
The Bennett Writing Center Team

format_metadata.subject: Your Writing Center Appointment Is Confirmed

questions.stem: According to the email, what should Marcus bring to his appointment?

options.A: A completed citation worksheet from a previous session.
options.B: A printed draft along with the assignment instructions.
options.C: A signed confirmation slip from the front desk staff.
options.D: A list of research topics approved by his instructor.

questions.explanation: The email asks Marcus to bring a printed copy of his draft along with the assignment instructions so the tutor can review the requirements with him.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about repeatedly missing scheduled sessions?

options.A: It allows students to reschedule freely within the same week.
options.B: It requires students to pay an additional booking deposit.
options.C: It could make booking near-future appointments harder.
options.D: It results in an immediate ban from the Writing Center.

questions.explanation: The email warns that late cancellations or no-shows may limit access to future same-week appointments, implying repeated missed sessions could make booking near-future appointments more difficult. An outright ban (D) overstates this.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To confirm a tutoring appointment and explain how to prepare.
options.B: To introduce a new tutor who has joined the Writing Center.
options.C: To remind students about the library's updated room numbers.
options.D: To outline the penalties for missing scheduled study sessions.

questions.explanation: The email confirms Marcus's appointment details and explains how to prepare, including what to bring and the cancellation policy. The other options capture only a single detail or an unstated idea.
